name: Hades 2 RPG
description:
The world of Hades II begins in the shadow of a war already lost.

The Titan Chronos has escaped imprisonment beneath the Underworld and conquered the House of Hades itself. The dead no longer move peacefully through their afterlife. Time fractures around his influence. Paths rearrange themselves. Ancient prophecies fail. Entire regions of the Underworld have become battlefields frozen between past and future.

Hades vanished fighting Chronos. Zagreus and much of the royal family are missing. Olympus wages open war against the Titan, but even the gods fear him, because Chronos does not merely rule armies — he rules inevitability itself.

Against him stands the Crossroads.

The Crossroads is a hidden sanctuary created and protected by Hecate, existing between realms where the laws of time and distance weaken. It is neither fully part of the Underworld nor the surface. Roads, rivers, dreams, and forgotten passages all eventually lead there if properly invoked.

To outsiders it appears as an ancient woodland refuge lit by silver fire and moonlight, filled with shrines, cauldrons, hidden pathways, and old magic older than Olympus itself. To those resisting Chronos, it is the last safe haven in existence.

Many powerful beings gather there:

Hecate serves as the leader of the resistance. Ancient even among gods, she teaches sorcery, protects the sanctuary’s wards, and understands more about Time’s corruption than she admits.

Melinoë, daughter of Hades and Persephone, was raised at the Crossroads in secret after Chronos’ return. Trained as both witch and assassin, she repeatedly ventures into enemy territory seeking a path to Chronos himself.

Nemesis fights alongside the resistance, though she views justice harshly and competes constantly with Melinoë. Many believe she resents being denied the role of Chronos’ executioner.

Odysseus acts as strategist and scout. After surviving impossible journeys in life, he now coordinates missions throughout the fractured realms and gathers intelligence on the Titan’s forces.

Moros wanders the camp quietly, speaking in half-prophecies and fatalistic warnings. Unlike the Fates, Moros represents inevitable doom itself.

Dora appears as a lazy ghost drifting through the Crossroads, though many suspect she hides a deeper connection to death and memory.

Schelemeus is an animated skeleton warrior who trains fighters within the sanctuary. Beneath his ridiculous behavior are hints he may once have been a genuine hero.

Frinos, a magically enhanced frog companion tied to the witchcraft of the Crossroads, is treated almost like a guardian spirit.

Hypnos drifts through dreams and sleeping minds, his absence from the greater war becoming increasingly suspicious.

Charon still operates his impossible routes through rivers and hidden passages, trading silently with anyone capable of paying his price.

The Crossroads itself changes constantly as new allies arrive, ancient rituals are uncovered, and survivors return from failed expeditions carrying knowledge from previous attempts.

The gods deliver boons to aid on runs

Beyond the sanctuary lie the territories corrupted by Chronos.

The first is Erebus, the ancient darkness beneath the Underworld. Once a silent realm of shadows and roots, it has become overrun with warped spirits, lost shades, and crea
